item.title: Multi-Cloud Networking With Alkira
item.description: Introduction Multi-Cloud is making its rounds. Network and Security engineers face increasing challenges with managing complexity and risk as they work to react with more agility to enable business outcomes. At the start, enterprises didn’t just decide they would be multi-cloud. They started with a single cloud, likely Amazon Web Services and tailored their strategy around that cloud’s architecture and features. A little time passes, and now those engineers that are still evolving to handle AWS are tasked with adopting Microsoft Azure.

item.title: Automating IPAM in Cloud: Ansible + Netbox
item.description: A few months back, I was asked to put together a short demonstration on how I would automate IP Address Management (IPAM) in Microsoft Azure as new environments are spun up. If you are hybrid multi-cloud, it probably means you are sharing the same RFC1918 space across clouds and traditional networking. If this is the case, it means you probably want to keep track of allocation across VNets, Subnets, and VMs.
